title: Mission to Prefsbelt IV
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Conflicting|the daytime mission in ''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron II: Rogue Leader]]''|the evening version of the same mission}} {{Battle |prev=[[Mission to Zhar]]''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]''{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=1545#16926420|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2007-09-26|time=5:34 PM}} |conc= |next=[[Raid on Bespin]]''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron II: Rogue Leader]]'' |image=[[File:Mission_to_Prefsbelt_IV.jpg]] |name=Mission to Prefsbelt IV |conflict=[[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] |date=[[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]'' places the [[Mission to Zhar]] shortly before the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]. |place=[[Prefsbelt IV/Legends|Prefsbelt IV]]''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron II: Rogue Leader: The Official Nintendo Player's Guide]]'' |result=*[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] victory **Shuttle [[Tydirium/Legends|''Tydirium'']] captured |side1=Alliance to Restore the Republic |side2=[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] |side3= |side4= |commanders1=*[[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] *[[General/Legends|General]] [[Crix Madine/Legends|Crix Madine]] |commanders2= |commanders3= |commanders4= |unit1= |unit2= |unit3= |unit4= |forces1=*[[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] **1 [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] **2 other starfighters *1 stolen [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighter]]''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron II: Rogue Leader]]'' 100% game completion *1 stolen [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class T-4a shuttle]] **''Tydirium'' |forces2=*TIE fighters *[[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE interceptors]] *[[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-AT Walkers]] *[[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-ST walkers]] *8 [[Sensor Outpost]]s *11 gun turrets |forces3= |forces4= |casual1= |casual2= |casual3= |casual4= |civilian=Portion of the facility staff and defense forces }} {{Quote|Attention cadets, the Academy will be locked down in four minutes.|An Imperial when Antilles attacks|}} The '''Mission to Prefsbelt IV''' took place when the Rebel pilot [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]], coordinated by [[General/Legends|General]] [[Crix Madine/Legends|Madine]], attempted to reclaim the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] [[Tydirium/Legends|''Tydirium'']]. Antilles infiltrated the [[Imperial Naval Academy/Legends|Academy]] through the southern mountain pass and avoided detection by the [[Sensor Outpost]]s. He then docked with the shuttle and took off toward the rendezvous point. After his escort arrived, he escaped. ==Prelude== The [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] required an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] to deliver a [[Commando/Legends|commando]] team on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Endor/Legends|Endor]]. In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], [[Rebel pilot/Legends|Rebel pilot]] [[Ace Azzameen/Legends|Ace Azzameen]] managed to capture the [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class shuttle]] [[Tydirium/Legends|''Tydirium'']] from [[Outpost 327]] during the [[Mission to Zhar]], but the Galactic Empire managed to recapture the shuttle soon after, and had it transferred to [[Prefsbelt IV/Legends|Prefsbelt IV]]. [[General/Legends|General]] [[Crix Madine/Legends|Crix Madine]] sent [[Rogue Leader/Legends|Rogue Leader]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] to the [[Imperial Naval Academy/Legends|Imperial Naval Academy]] on Prefsbelt IV to steal the ''Tydirium'' for Rebel use. ==Mission== Madine ordered Wedge Antilles to approach from the south through the mountain pass, and told him the shuttle was parked at the northern [[Imperial Academy/Legends|Academy platform]]. Madine also cautioned him to avoid the [[Sensor Outpost]]s. Antilles was able to avoid detection by the eight sensors and moved into the next section of the Academy. {{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: Rogue Squadron II: Rogue Leader}} He then abandoned his starfighter to steal a [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighter]] after eliminating the [[TIE pilot|pilot]]. Antilles then infiltrated the Academy. He then proceeded to the Academy, passing the landing platforms and other installations and the [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-AT]] and [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-ST defenses]]. Near the Academy, he strafed the rows of TIE starfighters and the gun turrets, revealing his presence. He also flew through one of the other landing pads and acquired a vehicular upgrade. {{Endgame}} The Academy staff announced that the Academy would go on lockdown in four minutes, and the fighters were deployed. Antilles knocked out the four turrets guarding the shuttle, docked on the landing pad, and transferred to the ''Tydirium.'' Pursued by TIE fighters and [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|interceptors]], he flew toward the rendezvous point, where three pursuing fighters were eliminated by Antilles' newly arrived escorts, two pilots from Rogue Squadron. Together they escaped the Academy. ==Aftermath== {{Quote|We have stolen a small Imperial shuttle. Disguised as a cargo ship, and using a secret Imperial code, a strike team will land on the moon and deactivate the shield generator.|[[General/Legends|General]] [[Crix Madine/Legends|Crix Madine]]|audio=Madine-Imperialshuttlestolen.flac|{{Film|VI}}}} Some time after the mission, Rogue Squadron joined in a [[Raid on Bespin|raid]] on the [[Tibanna/Legends|tibanna]] gas storage on [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]]. Alliance General [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] was able to use the ''Tydirium'' to infiltrate the [[Endor system/Legends|Endor system]] along with his commandos, prompting the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. ==Behind the scenes== {{Quote|Crix, I've got the shuttle! Mission accomplished.
Excellent, Rogue Group. We had to get that shuttle.|Antilles to Madine at the end of the night mission|}} There were 2 versions of the mission in the [[2001]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron II: Rogue Leader]]''; night and day, depending on the GameCube's clock. During the night, you pilot a [[T-47 airspeeder/Legends|T-47 airspeeder]] and during the day, a [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wing]]. *For the Night Mission, you had to evade the scanners under the dead of night in a Speeder. At the end, the player was chased by three TIEs, and escorted by two X-wings. *The Day Mission required Rogue Squadron to disable the scanners with their Y-Wings's [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannons]]. At the end, the player was chased by several TIES and two Y-wings arrive for escort. As there are two versions, this article incorporates only the information which appears in both missions, without specifying which version is official. If the player fails to capture the shuttle before the lockdown goes into effect, the shuttle will be lowered into the landing pad and the mission will fail. There are separate vehicle upgrades available in either version. For the daytime version, the player can acquire [[Advanced concussion missile]]s. The nighttime mission has the player acquiring [[Advanced spread proton bomb]]s instead. This article assumes the player picked it up during the mission. In the co-op campaign included in ''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike]]'', both players have to board the ''Tydirium'' before the player can use it. The first player is relegated to the pilot cockpit, whereas the second player will use its aft gun to shake off the pursuing TIEs. The player may unlock the TIE fighter by capturing it in both versions, and there are separate upgrades to be found in the versions. This article assumes the player captures the TIE fighter as part of the infiltration. In the day version, the player must hit the TIE with the ion cannon, and in the night version, the player must shoot the pilot near an Imperial outpost. The player may choose to not capture the TIE fighter and continue in their starfighter. The player may also destroy any Imperial target as long as they destroy the four turrets guarding the shuttle. The player can also maintain cover up until they reach the loading platform for the ''Tydirium'', with Wedge noting upon arrival that he can't steal the shuttle while the turrets surrounding the loading platform are still active. The [[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron II: Rogue Leader: The Official Nintendo Player's Guide|strategy guide]] recommends that the player conduct several pre-emptive attacks beforehand in order to gain a medal for the mission, and suggests using the Y-wing to do the attacks rather than the TIE fighter. The strategy guide also recommends that the player head straight to the rendezvous point, and this article follows that recommendation. Madine's opening dialogue is slightly different in each version. If the player steals the TIE fighter at night, there is a separate cutscene where Antilles picks up his speeder's gunner. "I've found him. We're on our way" is Antilles' alternate dialogue. There is no dialogue at the end of the day mission. The capture of the ''Tydirium'' was also featured in the [[1999]] video game ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]'', where it was stolen from an Imperial station by Ace Azzameen. The [[2007]] reference book ''[[Star Wars: Complete Cross-Sections]]'' attempted to reconcile the sources by saying that Antilles stole the shuttle while Azzameen acquired codes. [[Leland Chee]], the Keeper of [[Lucasfilm]]'s [[Holocron continuity database]], has stated that both thefts of the ''Tydirium'' occurred, and that the ''X-wing Alliance'' mission happened first. ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron II: Rogue Leader]]'' {{1st}} *''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike]]'' {{C|Co-op campaign only}} *{{Film|VI}} {{1stm}} *{{RJRadio|Pattern and Web}} {{Imo}} ==Sources== *[[Return of the Jedi: The National Public Radio Dramatization|''Return of the Jedi'': The National Public Radio Dramatization]] {{Imo}} *''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron II: Rogue Leader: The Official Nintendo Player's Guide]]'' *''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike: The Official Nintendo Player's Guide]]'' *''[[Star Wars: Complete Cross-Sections]]'' *{{StarshipsVehiclesCite|12|Welcome On Board}} *{{StarshipsVehiclesCite|22|Action Stations}} *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' {{Imo}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Schlacht von Prefsbelt IV |es=Misión en Prefsbelt IV }} {{GCW 4 ABY 1}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Prefsbelt IV, Mission to}} [[Category:Battles in the Prefsbelt sector]] [[Category:Heists and robberies]] [[Category:Missions of the Galactic Civil War]]
